values.value:
== APEX CLASS AND TRIGGER GENERATION ==
Apex files are NOT XML. They are source code deployed as part of a ZIP. Every Apex artifact requires EXACTLY TWO file entries in files[]: 1. The code file — metadataType ApexClass or ApexTrigger 2. The meta file — metadataType ApexClassMeta or ApexTriggerMeta
CRITICAL MAPPING — NEVER MIX THESE UP: ApexClass → .cls file → xmlContent = public class Foo { ... } (Apex source) ApexClassMeta → .cls-meta.xml file → xmlContent = <?xml ...><ApexClass>... (XML only) ApexTrigger → .trigger file → xmlContent = trigger Foo on Bar (...) { }(Apex source) ApexTriggerMeta → .trigger-meta.xml → xmlContent = <?xml ...><ApexTrigger>... (XML only) NEVER put <?xml into a .cls or .trigger file. NEVER put public class / trigger keywords into a -meta.xml file.
Meta file XML format: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <ApexClass xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <apiVersion>61.0</apiVersion> <status>Active</status> </ApexClass> (For trigger meta: use <ApexTrigger> root element instead of <ApexClass>.)
package.xml registration: - ONE member per class or trigger (both code and meta file share same member name) - Apex registers as: metadataType=ApexClass member=ClassName (NOT ApexClassMeta) - Apex registers as: metadataType=ApexTrigger member=TriggerName (NOT ApexTriggerMeta)
-- CREATE A NEW CLASS -- Generate four files[] entries: ApexClass classes/MyClass.cls (the source code) ApexClassMeta classes/MyClass.cls-meta.xml (the meta file)
-- UPDATE AN EXISTING CLASS -- 1. Read the full existing body from orgState.existingClass.body or orgState.existingHandlerClass.body 2. Add the new method(s) or change requested by the ticket 3. Output the COMPLETE class — ALL existing methods preserved, new method appended 4. Emit ApexClass + ApexClassMeta file entries (both files must be present) 5. NEVER emit only the new snippet — always emit the full class body
-- DELETE A CLASS OR TRIGGER -- Use destructiveFiles shorthand: { "metadataType": "ApexClass", "member": "MyClassName" } { "metadataType": "ApexTrigger", "member": "MyTriggerName" }
-- DEPLOYMENT PLAN FOR APEX -- Apex-only deploys always use scenario = "single". If the ticket also creates a new custom object: object in ZIP1, layout in ZIP2 — Apex always goes into ZIP1.
CORRECT 4-FILE PATTERN FOR TRIGGER + HANDLER: classes/LeadHandler.cls ← Apex CLASS source (public class LeadHandler {...}) classes/LeadHandler.cls-meta.xml ← XML meta only triggers/LeadTrigger.trigger ← Apex TRIGGER source (trigger LeadTrigger on Lead...) triggers/LeadTrigger.trigger-meta.xml ← XML meta only
NEVER PUT <?xml INTO A .cls OR .trigger FILE NEVER CREATE classes/XxxTrigger.cls FOR A TRIGGER NAMED XxxTrigger
Apex class and trigger file structure, 4-file pattern, create/update/delete
